commit.message:
Fix video transitions and add variable label to video frames (#16)

Transitions were invisible because captureStream(0)+requestFrame() is
unreliable — if requestFrame is unavailable the code fell back to a plain
700ms wait with no cross-fade at all.

New approach: captureStream(15) timed capture for all browsers.
Each fade/hold step draws to videoCanvas and waits 90ms (> 1/15fps = 67ms),
guaranteeing the 15fps stream samples the canvas before the next draw.

Also adds the variable name label (same style as PNG export) onto each
video frame so the viewer knows which variable is shown.

Fix frequency visualizer: align single-band mapping with dual-band spectrogram (#230)

The single-band log-frequency mapping (createLogFrequencyMapping) placed
frequencies at different output positions than the dual-band mapping
(createDualBandLogMapping) because it mapped over bin indices rather than
actual frequencies. This caused the scrubber's frequency visualization to
diverge from the spectrogram — e.g., A440 appeared at ~43% vs ~57%.

Introduce a shared REFERENCE_MIN_FREQUENCY anchor (2.5 Hz, derived from
the offline spectrogram's low-band configuration) that both mapping
functions use as the lower bound of the log scale. When binWidth is
provided to createLogFrequencyMapping, it uses frequency-aware mapping
aligned to this reference. FrequencyVisualizer's single-band paths now
pass binWidth so the scrubber inherently aligns with the spectrogram
without needing dual-band mode.

commit.message:
fix(ui-compiler): exclude signal API vars from computed classification (#909)

* fix(ui-compiler): exclude signal API vars from computed classification [#906]

Signal API variables (form(), query(), createLoader()) are constructor calls
that create stateful instances. When they reference other signal API vars
through closures (e.g., form() with onSuccess referencing query().refetch()),
the analyzer incorrectly classified them as computed, wrapping them in
computed(). This re-created the instance on every evaluation, losing internal
state, and caused __bindElement to be undefined at runtime because the JSX
transformer's generated code was never processed by the computed read transform.

The fix adds `if (signalApiVars.has(name)) continue;` to the computed
classification loop, ensuring signal API vars always stay as 'static'.

Closes #906

Add subscription protection, blocklist removal detection, and unblock policy

Subscription protection:
- Fetches subscriptions from youtube.com/feed/channels at the start of each run
- Skips blocking any channel the user is subscribed to
- Logs a one-time WARNING when a subscribed channel appears in the blocklist
  (stored in state["would_have_blocked"], never repeats); visible via --stats

Blocklist removal detection:
- Tracks which source blocked each channel in state["blocked_by"]
- On each run, checks for channels dropped from the current source's list
- Logs a prominent WARNING and removes from processed state so the channel
  can appear in recommendations again (possible false positive correction)

--unblock-policy {all,any} (default: all):
- "all": only unblock when dropped from every source that originally blocked it
- "any": unblock as soon as any single source drops the channel

State schema is backward compatible; missing fields added on load.
7 new tests for check_removals + backward compat; 55/55 passing.

commit.message:
Fix OCR: auto-detect grid bounds + let Tesseract handle binarization

Root cause of missed digits: (1) grid not perfectly filling the image
caused misaligned cell extraction (row 1 entirely missed), (2) our
manual thresholding was worse than Tesseract's built-in binarization.

- Auto-detect grid boundary by scanning for rows/cols with high
  dark-pixel density (grid lines), then extract cells relative to
  the detected grid — fixes alignment issues
- Send contrast-enhanced grayscale directly to Tesseract instead of
  manually thresholding — Tesseract v5 has superior internal Otsu
- Simple empty detection: contrast range < 20 = uniform = empty
- Larger cells (140px + 30px padding) for better resolution
- Lower confidence threshold (15) to catch more valid digits

* fix: account balance display and ETH existence detection

Two root causes:

(a) Aptos showing "0.0" before account exists:
- The single try-catch wrapped both getAccountInfo and getAccountAPTAmount,
  so a balance fetch failure incorrectly set aptAccountExists=false, and
  vice versa a silent 0 return from getAccountAPTAmount set it to true.

  existence failure.
- Display: show "Account not yet on chain" when apt === 0 even if
  aptAccountExists is true (SDK may return 0 silently for missing accounts).
- Fixed broken backoff condition (snapshot.loading is always false after
  fetchSnapshot resolves).

(b) ETH balances not updating after faucet:
- ETH faucet mints FakeETH/FakeUSD (ERC-20) without touching native ETH
  balance or nonce. ethAccountExists = nonce > 0 || ethBalance > 0n was
  always false after minting, hiding balances behind "Account not yet on
  chain" even though the polling was fetching correct values.

Fix card drag teleport: reactive drop resolution via useLayoutEffect

Previous approaches (rAF, flushSync) all failed because the parent
component provides style.left via props, and its re-render from the
React Query cache update is decoupled from EntryCard's local state.
This created an unpaintable intermediate frame where the new layout
position AND old drag transform were both applied.

New approach: after dropping, keep isDragging=true (holding the drag
transform in place) and store the expected new date in a ref. A
useLayoutEffect checks every render whether entry.dateSent matches
the pending date. When the parent finally re-renders with the updated
layout, the effect fires BEFORE the browser paints, clears the drag
state, and hands the captured drop rect to the FLIP animation. The
synchronous re-render from setState-in-useLayoutEffect ensures no

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: split PTX compilation to isolate insert register allocation

Warp-coop intrinsics (__ballot_sync, __shfl_sync) in query kernels
caused NVRTC to increase register pressure module-wide, spilling the
insert kernel's 512-byte local arrays to slow local memory (10x
regression). Fix: compile insert and query kernels as separate PTX
modules via #define COMPILE_INSERT / COMPILE_QUERY guards. Bump v0.4.1.

commit.message:
fix(core): return VerifyResult from crypto verification instead of bare boolean (#83) (#125)

Previously, all verification paths (Ed25519, KMS) returned bare booleans
and silently swallowed errors in catch blocks. This made it impossible to
distinguish between "signature is cryptographically invalid" and "verification
failed due to a network/service error".

- Add VerifyResult type { valid: boolean; error?: string }
- Update Verifier interface to return Promise<VerifyResult>
- Update Ed25519Verifier, KmsVerifier, and standalone verify functions
- Update verify-dossier.ts to differentiate error vs invalid signature
- Export VerifyResult from @ai-dossier/core
